results.recommendation: Add a Compositions subsection to Core Concepts in SKILL.md with a brief example (e.g., Items : Composition of many OrderItems). Also add @mandatory to the Common Aspects or annotations section. Both are fundamental CDS patterns that should appear in the quick-reference surface.

results.gaps: SKILL.md itself has zero coverage of xs-security.json structure. The Authorization Examples section (lines 269-280) only shows CDS-level annotations (@requires, @restrict) but does not mention role-templates, scope-references, or $XSAPPNAME. results.recommendation: Add a brief XSUAA Configuration subsection under Authorization Examples in SKILL.md showing the key xs-security.json structure (scopes, role-templates, scope-references with $XSAPPNAME). This is a critical deployment artifact that developers need to understand, not just copy from a template.

improvement_recommendations: Add Composition examples to SKILL.md Core Concepts section — Compositions are fundamental to CDS data modeling (parent-child relationships) and currently only exist in references/cdl-syntax.md, not the main skill document.
Add @mandatory annotation to SKILL.md — this is a commonly used annotation for required fields, currently only in references/cdl-syntax.md and references/annotations-reference.md.

Add xs-security.json structure overview to SKILL.md Authorization section — the current authorization coverage only shows CDS annotations but omits the XSUAA descriptor format (role-templates, scopes, $XSAPPNAME) which is essential for BTP deployment.

Add a bound action example to SKILL.md Basic Service section — only unbound actions are shown in the main document, while bound actions (entity-level) are a very common pattern covered only in references.

Consider adding a 'Common Patterns' quick-reference section to SKILL.md that briefly surfaces the most-used patterns from references (Compositions, bound actions, XSUAA config) with pointers to the full reference files.
